title: The Commander Battles Intensify
description: On pages 26–29, this part focuses on Unknown person #25, Unknown person #26, Unknown person #27, Yamato, Unknown person #28, King as The Skull Dome battles reach their final forms as Sanji, Zoro, Kid, Law, Yamato, and the alliance push back against the emperors and their commanders.
scene: The Skull Dome battles reach their final forms as Sanji, Zoro, Kid, Law, Yamato, and the alliance push back against the emperors and their commanders.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They say when a devil fruit user dies...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...their fruit will re-appear somewhere in the world.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: Don't talk about something so disturb- Bing.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's no ability someone could want enough to kill a crewmate.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: Who're you calling a crew- mate!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: Ah, sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's just a matter of time though, right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: Hey!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: By the way, on this ship there's a rule that whoever finds a fruit can eat it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...why? Do you have a fruit you want to eat?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: ...I do.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: Nah, not really.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: Wanna Hear about it?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: Haha,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #25
dialogue.text: teach...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: Is it that fruit?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: Of course,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even if it takes my whole life, I wanna find it.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: The clear-clear fruit that makes you invisible!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: We're out of booze.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: Can I open this barrel?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: Wait, wait, you can't open that one!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: That's pops' booze!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: But this is the cheap stuff, right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: It's pops' favour- Rite.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: Really? An empe- ROR likes this?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Since pops sends his share of the treasure back to his home town,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: He prefers sticking to the cheap stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: I hear it's a piss- poor village.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They can't even pay their heavenly tribute to the celestial dragons.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: The world govern- went won't protect them.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's the same for the island we just saved.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #26
dialogue.text: That's why he

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's why he lends them our flag instead.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #27
dialogue.text: Our pops, he's just...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #27
dialogue.text: ...that kind of pirate.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't care if they call me a villain!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #27
dialogue.text: I'll make the whole world ...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #27
dialogue.text: ...know my name!!

dialogue.speaker: Yamato
dialogue.text: He's a great person, right ace!?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The difference between you and him is like night and day! A firefly compared to the sun!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #28
dialogue.text: Quit agitating him, teach.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #28
dialogue.text: YAA!! ya!!

summary: This part advances the Wano / Onigashima War through 43 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on battle momentum, shifting alliances, and character resolve.
